# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v1

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          python -m unittest discover -s tests

      - name: Deploy
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v0
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.DEPLOYMENT_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.DEPLOYMENT_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.DEPLOYMENT_PASSWORD }}
          script: |
            cd /var/www/myapp
            git add .
            git commit -m "CI/CD"
            git push origin main
